channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump takes swipe at Biden, says US will support Somalia against Houthis
item.description: President Trump warned Houthis to hide after removing red tape and empowering the U.S. military to protect the people of Somalia from terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Results of President Trump's physical released, here's what they say
item.description: President Trump is in "excellent health," the White House physician said in a release following the president's annual physical at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump team tries to project confidence and calm after his tariff moves rattled markets
item.description: White House advisers and Cabinet members are trying to project confidence and calm as they defend President Donald Trump’s economic policies after another week of reeling markets that saw the Republican administration reverse course on some of its steepest tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: State Department wants staff to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias during Biden's term
item.description: As the Christian world commemorates Holy Week leading up to Easter Sunday, the State Department has issued an appeal for its employees to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias, including actions taken for opposition to vaccines or personal pronoun choice, that may have occurred during form
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump goes with his gut and the world goes along for the ride
item.description: President Donald Trump likes keeping people on edge waiting for his next move, and that's been clear when it comes to his tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary protected status for thousands of Afghans amid deportation push
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security doesn't plan to renew the protected status of thousands of Afghans who fled the country in 2021, when the U.S. withdrew and the Taliban took over.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP frustrations with conservatives rise after budget fight
item.description: Frustrations are bubbling up among House Republicans after the conference — by the skin of its teeth — overcame internal disputes to adopt a framework for President Trump’s legislative agenda, a troubling sign for the group as it heads into the next, more difficult, step in achieving the president’s
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Longtime House Republican weighs run to flip Dem-held Senate seat in key battleground
item.description: Longtime Republican Rep. Bill Huizenga of Michigan is weighing a run in the 2026 midterm elections for Michigan's open Senate seat, which the GOP is aiming to flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump proclaims himself 'in good shape,' but the results of his physical aren't immediately released
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump had an annual physical Friday and concluded, “I did well,” praising his own heart, soul and cognitive ability while noting medical reports from White House doctors may not be ready until the weekend. The 78-year-old, who in January became the oldest in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Washington, DC, faces budget crisis after House leaves $1.1 billion hole in city coffers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the House of Representatives adjourned for a two-week recess Thursday, it left Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens sanctions, tariffs on Mexico in water dispute
item.description: Under a 1944 treaty, Mexico must send 1.75 million acre-feet of water to the U.S. from the Rio Grande.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: White House responds to Supreme Court ruling on mistaken deportation
item.description: The White House on Friday responded to the Supreme Court’s ruling that the Trump administration must “facilitate” the return of a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rather than "effectuate" the return.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge scolds DOJ for demanding delay in mistakenly deported man case
item.description: The federal judge who ordered the Trump administration to try to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, scolded the Justice Department for demanding more time to update the court on next steps.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security lists thousands of living immigrants as dead to prompt them to leave, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has moved to classify more than 6,000 living immigrants as dead, canceling their Social Security numbers and effectively wiping out their ability to work or receive benefits in an effort to get them to leave the country, according to two people familiar wit
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must work to bring back mistakenly deported Maryland man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must work to bring back a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to prison in El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Federal jobs watch: Just-released February jobs report shows few losses in Minnesota
item.description: The federal workforce includes nearly 34,000 jobs in Minnesota. Over 20,000 are located in the Twin Cities metropolitan area. The Bureau of Labor Statistics’ month jobs report shows a loss of 100 federal jobs through Minnesota from January to February.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Gawthrop and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature moving bills as Friday deadline nears; ghost kitchens, open enrollment among winners
item.description: Up against a Friday deadline for bills to be passed out of committee, Nevada lawmakers are working this week to move mountains of legislation in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: “In his short time in government, Elon Musk has done enormous harm to working Americans,” House Democrats said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where did U.S. humanities grants go? To projects from a baseball film to AI research
item.description: From AI research to historical preservation, programs funded by grants from the National Endowment for the Humanities reach every corner of the U.S. Now the government has terminated those grants.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair, MPR News

item.title: As measles spreads, federal budget cuts force closure of vaccine clinics
item.description: Federal funding cuts, though temporarily blocked by a judge, have upended vaccination outreach across the country, including in Arizona, Minnesota, Nevada, Texas, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith, Arielle Zionts, and Jackie Fortiér, MPR News

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5
